title: Founding a Hardware Simulation SaaS Platform @ Sahara Cloud

blocks.text: Co-founded Sahara Cloud, a web-based hardware simulation and remote testing platform designed to help engineering teams build, test, and debug hardware systems in the cloud. Led product strategy, customer discovery, MVP definition, fundraising, and early go-to-market execution, raising $750K in pre-seed funding and securing early pilots across universities and enterprise customers.
Problem
Hardware engineering teams faced slow, fragmented, and expensive development workflows.
Software, firmware, and hardware testing often happened across disconnected tools and environments.
Teams relied on makeshift scripts and manual workarounds to connect simulation, debugging, and testing workflows.
Limited access to physical hardware created bottlenecks, high capital costs, and slower development cycles.
Debugging hardware issues was time-consuming, making it harder for teams to reduce R&D cost and time-to-market.
My Role
As Co-founder and Chief Product Officer, I led product discovery, MVP strategy, roadmap development, fundraising support, customer development, and early go-to-market execution.
Solution
Built a cloud-based platform that enabled engineering teams to run hardware simulations, remote debugging workflows, and testing environments through a shared web interface.
Remote hardware access
Cloud-based simulation workflows
Debugging and unit testing support
Shared testing environments for engineering teams
Early onboarding flows for academic and enterprise pilots
Execution / Product Decisions
Conducted 50+ customer discovery interviews with engineering teams, universities, and enterprise prospects to validate workflow pain points.
Defined the MVP around the most painful early use cases: remote access, simulation setup, debugging, and testing workflow visibility.
Launched beta with 200 users, 3 universities, and 1 enterprise client.
Built strategic partnerships with academic institutions and enterprise clients to validate demand.
Used A/B testing and behavioral insights to improve sign-up conversion by 50%.
Drove a 30% prospect-to-demo rate through clearer positioning and targeted outreach.
Supported fundraising with customer evidence, market validation, and product roadmap clarity.
Tradeoffs & Product Decisions
Breadth vs focus: Narrowed the MVP around remote testing and simulation workflows instead of trying to replace every hardware design tool.
Academic vs enterprise GTM: Used universities for faster validation while pursuing enterprise pilots for higher-value commercial proof.
Platform ambition vs MVP speed: Built only the workflows needed to validate usage, technical feasibility, and willingness to engage.
Custom pilot needs vs scalable platform: Supported early pilot requirements while identifying repeatable patterns for a broader SaaS product.
Results & Impact
Raised $750K in pre-seed funding to support product development and early go-to-market.
Launched MVP with 200 beta users, 3 universities, and 1 enterprise client.
Secured 4 pilot engagements across academic and enterprise customers.
Achieved a 30% prospect-to-demo rate through improved positioning and targeted outreach.
Increased sign-up conversion by 50% through A/B testing and onboarding improvements.
Modeled a potential 35% reduction in R&D development time and up to $1M in customer cost savings based on pilot workflows and customer validation.
Metrics Tracked
Beta users
Pilot engagements
Prospect-to-demo conversion rate
Sign-up conversion rate
Customer discovery interviews conducted
Modeled R&D time savings
Fundraising milestones
Key Learnings
Founder-led product development requires narrowing a large market problem into a painful wedge that customers will validate quickly.
Customer discovery is most useful when it reveals repeated workflow bottlenecks, not just general dissatisfaction with existing tools.
Early pilots need to balance custom support with the discipline of identifying repeatable SaaS patterns.
Fundraising is stronger when product vision is backed by evidence from real users, pilots, and measurable workflow pain.
Visual Diagrams

Hardware Development Workflow — Traditional: local tools → manual scripts → physical hardware bottlenecks → delayed debugging. Sahara: cloud simulation → remote access → shared testing → faster validation.
MVP Validation Funnel — Discovery interviews → beta users → university pilots → enterprise pilot → investor validation.
Product Wedge — Remote access + simulation + debugging as the initial wedge before broader hardware development platform expansion.
